vulnerabilities.description: Netty verifyContinuationFrame fails to limit the number of zero-byte CONTINUATION frames, bypassing maxHeaderListSize and causing excessive CPU consumption on the server.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: No action required. Liquibase does not run an HTTP/2 server.

ratings.justification: Server-side HTTP/2 vulnerability. Liquibase never accepts inbound HTTP/2 connections; Netty is only used as a client transport.

analysis.detail: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood DoS in Netty HTTP/2 codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport. There is no inbound HTTP/2 listener.

Netty HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame validation bypass in codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport.
